draft.introduction: The Garuda Purana is a Vaishnava text dealing with cosmology, dharma, and especially the afterlife and funeral rites. It is traditionally read during Hindu mourning periods and contains the most detailed Hindu account of death and the journey of the soul.

translation.text: Garuda said: ‘O Lord Vishnu, tell me about the nature of death, the journey of the soul after death, and the rites that liberate the departed.’ The Lord said: ‘Listen, O Garuda. At the time of death, the soul departs through one of the body’s openings. Those who have lived righteously ascend; the unrighteous descend. The rites performed by the living aid the dead.’

translator_notes.note: The Garuda Purana’s detailed afterlife geography—describing the soul’s journey through various realms—parallels the Tibetan Book of the Dead and the Egyptian Book of the Dead. Its emphasis on the living’s ability to aid the dead through ritual reflects the Hindu understanding of interconnected cosmic obligation.
